scenarios.name: Terminal renders on homepage between How It Works and Garden Stats
Terminal reflects latest published run data (winner title, candidate count, feedback count)
Terminal renders expected DOM structure (dots, title, 5 lines, 5 prompts, 1 highlight)
Terminal section is hidden by default in raw HTML
Terminal section header shows correct copy (The Command Line / Latest Run)
AC-1: #terminal-section appears between How It Works and #garden-stats as a direct sibling
AC-2: terminal body contains the winner title from decision.json
AC-2: terminal body shows candidate count from decision.candidates.length
AC-2: terminal body shows feedback totalItems from feedback-digest.json
AC-2: terminal highlight contains winner title and averageScore
AC-3: titlebar contains exactly 3 terminal dot elements
AC-3: terminal title contains the date 2026-04-10
AC-3: terminal body has 5 lines with 5 prompts
AC-3: the 5 pipeline stages are explore, score, build, test, ship
Judge panel includes claude, gpt, and gemini model families
Score line contains a single highlight with winner title and score
Accessibility: aria-labelledby points to Latest Run heading
Accessibility: terminal dots are aria-hidden and not focusable
Accessibility: design-token colors match expected values
Accessibility: keyboard focus is not trapped in terminal
Mobile: terminal fills available width with no large margins
Mobile: no horizontal scrollbar at 375px viewport
Mobile: font-size matches --text-xs at <640px
Mobile: terminal dot width is 10px at <640px
Mobile: long text wraps via overflow-wrap without overflow
Desktop: terminal container has max-width 720px and is centered
Desktop: no horizontal scrollbar at 1024px viewport
Desktop: font-size is --text-sm at >=640px
Desktop: terminal dot width is 12px at >=640px
Design system: spec requires design-system tokens and no new terminal tokens added
Design system: terminal CSS rules use no hardcoded hex or rgba color values
